TSLA: ExxonMobil moves to reincorporate in Texas from New Jersey. ExxonMobil is planning to move its legal headquarters from New Jersey to Texas, citing Texas's business-friendly environment and legal reforms. This move, recommended by the company's board of directors, aligns its legal home with its operational base in Spring, Houston, for the first time since 1882. Shareholders will vote on the redomiciliation on May 27, which follows similar moves by other major companies like Tesla and Coinbase.
TGT: Dollar General Q4 2025 Earnings Preview & Market Expectations - News and Statistics. Dollar General (NYSE:DG) is set to announce its Q4 2025 financial results before the market opens, with analysts projecting a revenue increase similar to the previous year. The company's prior quarter matched revenue forecasts and surpassed profit and EBITDA estimates. Despite recent volatility in the non-discretionary retail sector, Dollar General's stock price has remained stable, and the average analyst price target is slightly above its current trading price.

Earnings To Watch: Zumiez (ZUMZ) Reports Q4 Results Tomorrow. Zumiez (ZUMZ) is set to report its Q4 earnings on Thursday after market close, with analysts expecting revenue growth of 3.6% year over year. The company beat revenue expectations last quarter, and its peers like Victoria's Secret and Urban Outfitters have shown mixed results in their recent Q4 reports. Zumiez's stock is down 6.5% over the last month and is heading into earnings with an average analyst price target slightly above its current share price.
SBUX: Starbucks eyes 250K-square-foot lease at downtown development. Starbucks is reportedly considering a 250,000-square-foot office lease at the Peabody Union development in downtown Nashville, which could be one of the largest office deals in the city in years. The Fortune 500 company is actively seeking a massive office space in the area. This move is part of earlier plans for Starbucks to open a corporate office in Nashville.

Wolfe Research Turns Neutral on Starbucks (SBUX) amid Competitive Coffee Market. Wolfe Research downgraded Starbucks (SBUX) to Peer Perform from Outperform, citing the need for sustained execution evidence in its turnaround and an increasingly competitive coffee market. Despite Starbucks' strong brand, pricing power, and global scale, the firm wants to see more consistent results from its "Back to Starbucks" plan. The article also notes that while Starbucks continues to expand and grow revenue, some investors believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside and less risk.

Lee: Tennessee offers Starbucks 'very different' business environment. Tennessee Governor Bill Lee announced that Starbucks plans to open a corporate office in Nashville later this year, hinting at a significant number of jobs. Lee emphasized that Tennessee offers Starbucks a "very different" business environment compared to its current Seattle base. The announcement followed his address to the Nashville Area Chamber of Commerce.
DIS: Capital International Investors Grows Stake in The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Capital International Investors has increased its stake in The Walt Disney Company by 0.4%, bringing its total holding to 5,148,071 shares valued at approximately $589.45 million. Despite Disney beating quarterly earnings and revenue estimates, the near-term outlook is mixed due to strong box office performance for its new Pixar film "Hoppers" contrasted with increasing ad-market competition and geopolitical travel risks. Analysts currently have a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating for DIS, with an average target price of $135.80.

Banque Cantonale Vaudoise Cuts Position in The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Banque Cantonale Vaudoise reduced its stake in The Walt Disney Company by 21.5% in the third quarter, selling 9,523 shares and leaving it with 34,817 shares valued at approximately $3.99 million. The company's stock currently trades around $101, with analysts giving it a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating and an average target price of $135.80. Disney recently beat EPS estimates with $1.63 and reported $25.98 billion in revenue, highlighting some positive performance despite market pressures.

Disney Names Thomas Mazloum As New Head Of Experiences. Thomas Mazloum has been appointed Chairman, Disney Experiences, taking over from Josh D’Amaro, who became CEO of The Walt Disney Co. In this role, Mazloum will oversee Disney's global portfolio of businesses, including theme parks, cruise ships, resorts, and consumer products. This promotion follows his successful tenure as President of Disneyland Resort and comes as Disney plans to invest $60 billion in its Experiences segment over the next decade.

Disney Experiences Announces Leadership Changes. Disney Experiences has announced significant leadership changes, with Thomas Mazloum succeeding Josh D’Amaro as Chairman of Disney Experiences. Mazloum, bringing extensive operational expertise and international leadership, will oversee Disney's global theme parks, cruise lines, resorts, and consumer products. This restructuring also includes new roles for Jill Estorino as President of Disneyland Resort, Tasia Filippatos as President of Disney Parks International, and Lisa Baldzicki as President of Disney Consumer Products, aiming to drive growth and enhance guest experiences globally.

Josh D’Amaro Sets New Leadership at Disney’s Lucrative Experiences Division. The Walt Disney Co. has appointed Thomas Mazloum, current president of the Disneyland Resort, as the new chairman of Disney Experiences, succeeding Josh D’Amaro. D'Amaro will become Disney’s CEO next week. This leadership change also involves new roles for Jill Estorino at Disneyland Resort, Tasia Filippatos at Disney Parks International, and Lisa Baldzicki at Disney Consumer Products, as Disney continues to invest heavily in its highly profitable experiences division.

Disney Cruise Line Makes Asia Debut with Maiden Voyage of ‘Disney Adventure’. Disney Cruise Line is expanding its global presence with the maiden voyage of the Disney Adventure from Singapore, marking its first ship in Asia. The company sees this as a significant step to introduce new guests to Disney Experiences, with most passengers being first-time cruisers and over 80% of sailings already booked for fiscal year 2026. The Disney Adventure, the largest ship in the fleet, offers unique immersive storytelling experiences inspired by Disney, Marvel, and Pixar, tailored with regional influences.
NKE: What's Going On With Nike Stock Today?. Nike Inc. stock gained in premarket trading on Tuesday following news of a new $1 billion credit line and a $300 million restructuring plan. Analysts maintain a "Buy" rating with an average price target of $79.70 ahead of its Mar. 31 earnings report, where EPS is estimated at 30 cents and revenue at $11.25 billion.
